stage_prompt.name: Hardening — Split Doctrine, Soak, Fleet Handoff

comprehension_gate.rule: MANDATORY FIRST ACTION. Present structured summary. STOP and WAIT for Lead Architect approval.

item.name: DO NOT split CLAUDE.md / copilot-instructions.md as part of v2 execution
item.description: v1 items 6.1–6.3 prescribe splitting these doctrine files. **Lead Architect note (2026-04-24): SOP work has already been done on these files in main.** Defer the split until a separate, explicitly-approved session.
item.action: Mark 6.1, 6.2, 6.3 as DEFERRED in STATUS.md when this stage runs. Skip them in the v2 pass; revisit after soak.

item.name: Cross-repo reference rules
item.description: Once the split is eventually done, both repos must reference each other deterministically. Codify the rules:

rules.r: devplatform tools resolve target project via `--project-root` (CLI) or env BLARAI_ROOT (PowerShell). NO hardcoded BlarAI paths.
BlarAI/.platform/registry.yaml maps logical project_id → absolute path. devplatform reads this on startup.
devplatform/projects/registry.yaml maps project_id → Vikunja project_id (locked at id=8 for fleet_reports).
Two-repo fleet-hygiene.md (devplatform/docs/governance/fleet-hygiene.md) governs both repos. BlarAI keeps a thin pointer.

item.name: Soak monitoring — escalation-watchdog liveness and bridge daemon liveness
item.description: v1 item 6.7 monitors generic scheduled tasks and Vikunja DB. v2 reaffirms `Escalation Watchdog` (already part of the live 13-task fleet) is included in the soak set and adds an explicit bridge daemon process check. NOTE: Get-Process has no CommandLine property — must use Get-CimInstance Win32_Process for command-line filtering.

monitoring_additions.cmd: Get-CimInstance Win32_Process -Filter "Name = 'python.exe'" | Where-Object { $_.CommandLine -match 'tools\.vikunja_mcp\.bridge' } | Select-Object ProcessId, CommandLine

stage_prompt.objective:
Finalize the separation by splitting commingled doctrine documents (CLAUDE.md, copilot-instructions.md) between BlarAI and devplatform, completing the 24-hour soak, and optionally handing off further platform maintenance to the fleet now that it operates from devplatform.
Unlike earlier stages, this one is LOW risk and CAN span multiple sessions.

preconditions.check: Fleet has run 5+ scheduled invocations successfully from devplatform.

item.name: Split CLAUDE.md
item.description: Current `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\CLAUDE.md` mixes BlarAI product context with platform agent-operating-model doctrine. Split into: - **`C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\CLAUDE.md`** — keep: project identity, architecture summary, phase history, locked decisions, active state, coding standards. Replace Vikunja+fleet sections with a short pointer ("Platform machinery lives at C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform — see devplatform/CLAUDE.md"). - **`C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\CLAUDE.md`** — contains: Vikunja conventions, MCP tools list, bridge protocol, agent operating model, SDV/SCR/SWAGR workflow, DEC-15 sprint pattern.

item.name: Split .github/copilot-instructions.md
item.description: Same pattern: - BlarAI copy retains: project_vision, phase_directives, hardware_and_determinism, security_and_workflow_constraints, infrastructure_prerequisites (Hyper-V VM provisioning), coding_standards. - devplatform copy retains: core_operating_principles, chat_role_taxonomy, interaction_rules, vikunja_task_tracking. Each file gets a "pointer to its peer" section. Note: infrastructure_prerequisites describes BlarAI's Hyper-V Orchestrator VM and is BlarAI-product-specific — must stay with BlarAI.

item.name: Split AGENTS.md
item.description: BlarAI AGENTS.md becomes a thin pointer. devplatform AGENTS.md holds the real content.

item.name: Update devplatform pyproject.toml to its final state
item.description: Remove placeholder bits from Stage 1.7 / Stage 3.4. Add proper version, description, author.

item.name: Migrate remaining platform-layer docs to devplatform/docs/
item.description: Any doctrine docs missed in Stage 3.2 or Stage 5.5 — sweep and relocate.

item.name: Commit doctrine splits
item.command: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git add CLAUDE.md AGENTS.md .github/copilot-instructions.md; git commit -m "Stage 6: split doctrine — BlarAI retains product context, devplatform owns platform doctrine"; cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; git add -A; git commit -m "Stage 6: finalize platform doctrine"

item.name: 24-hour soak observation
item.description: Let the fleet run for 24 hours on devplatform. Monitor: - Scheduled task last-run results (all should be 0) - Vikunja DB size growth (should increase normally with tasks) - Bridge daemon liveness - Any unexpected BlarAI directory access by fleet tools

monitoring_commands.cmd: Get-ScheduledTask -TaskPath '\BlarAI\' | Get-ScheduledTaskInfo | Select TaskName, LastRunTime, LastTaskResult

item.name: Auto-create hardening tickets for residual smells (S-1, S-3)
item.description: WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md flagged two non-blocking architectural smells that were intentionally deferred past cutover. Create Vikunja tickets so they are not lost.

ticket.title: S-1: Rename `blarai_next_task_resolver.py` to project-agnostic name
ticket.body: Post-platform-separation hardening. The fleet tool `tools/fleet_ops/blarai_next_task_resolver.py` (now under devplatform) hardcodes BlarAI in its identifier even though Stage 2.4.v2 added the `--blarai-root` flag and Stage 2.5.v2 made the resolver project-agnostic in behavior. Rename to `next_task_resolver.py` (or `project_next_task_resolver.py`) and update all imports. Reference: docs/platform_separation/WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md §S-1.

ticket.title: S-3: Template `action_generator.py:82` rollback example from --project-root
ticket.body: `tools/fleet_ops/action_generator.py` line 82 emits a Vikunja audit-task description containing a hardcoded `git -C 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI' revert -m 1 ...` rollback command. Non-blocking in single-target reality, but should be templated from the `--project-root` argument so that multi-project fleet operation produces correct rollback commands per-target. Reference: docs/platform_separation/WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md Appendix A.1 / S-3.

item.command: # Use the bridge or MCP CLI from devplatform (post-cutover) to create both tickets. # Replace --project-id with DevPlatform-Meta project id (captured in Stage 1.4 evidence). cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1 python tools/vikunja_mcp/cli.py create-task --project-id $DEVPLATFORM_META_PID ` --title "S-1: Rename blarai_next_task_resolver.py to project-agnostic name" --priority 2 python tools/vikunja_mcp/cli.py create-task --project-id $DEVPLATFORM_META_PID ` --title "S-3: Template action_generator.py:82 rollback example from --project-root" --priority 1
item.verify: Both tickets visible in Vikunja UI under DevPlatform-Meta project.

item.name: Optional: hand off further platform maintenance to the fleet
item.description: Now that the fleet operates from devplatform, it CAN be used for further platform work (e.g., future refactors, dependency upgrades). The bootstrap paradox is resolved — the fleet no longer lives where it's working. Decision is Lead Architect's; if yes, create a Vikunja task in DevPlatform-Meta project kicking off the first fleet-owned devplatform work item.

item.name: Archive the platform_separation docs
item.description: After soak completes cleanly, move `docs/platform_separation/` to `docs/archive/platform_separation/` in BlarAI. Keep for permanent audit reference.

item.name: Final STATUS.md update and close
item.description: Mark Stage 6 COMPLETE. Record soak-test metrics. Append a "Migration Complete" section summarizing final state.

steps.step: Revert the doctrine split commits (git revert on both repos).
Keep investigating — don't panic-rollback the whole migration. Stages 1–5 should be stable at this point.

verification_commands_for_lead_architect.cmd: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git log --oneline -5; cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform; git log --oneline -5

exit_criteria.criterion: CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, copilot-instructions.md split cleanly between the two repos.
BlarAI/docs/ contains no platform-doctrine files (only product docs).
24-hour soak passed: all scheduled task runs succeeded, Vikunja DB healthy, no regressions.
